CHAP. Iii . of the straight account we m•st give unto God at the day of judgement . to draw every man to a better consideration of his time , and of his account day , our saviour add a reason why we shall be ready , and always prepare to render our account ; Matth. 4. for ( say he ) the kingdom of God be at hand . what will move a man to consider of himself , and of his account day , if this will not ? when he hear that the same be at hand . mortal man in their account they make to their lord and master , will gather their scroll , bill , paper , and other write , from hundredth to farthing , lest they be find unworthy man to be put in trust with such worldly commodity . if mortal man have such a care how to make their account to mortal auditor , what care , nay , what fear ought to be in all man and woman , when they be summon by the eternal God to appear before he and his angel , to give account how they have spend their time from their birth until their die day ? here we be summode to the high imperial court of eternal doom . the time be come ; mark . 1.15 and the kingdom of God be at hand . oh lord who shall escape amerce here ? nay , who shall escape damnation here ? seek up your scroll , search the book of your conscience : we be call to the court , where every man conscience shall be lay open . wherefore unless we repent ; that our sin may be blot out with the blood of Christ Jesus , the lord will call we to account , the book of our conscience shall be lay open , and every sin both secret and know shall be bring to light : and not one sin shall pass , but all our sin , both past , present , and to come , as well our rebellious thought , rev. 10.12 as sinful act commit . whereupon saint Bernard say , omne tempus tibi impensum requiretur a te qualiter fuerit expensum : Bernard , all the time that God have give thou ( except thou repent ) shall be require at thy hand , how thou have spend it . there shall inquisition be make for the thought of the ungodly : there shall not a wicked thought pass in judgement . Wisd. 1.9 . chap IV. now follow the manner of this account . i . who must give account ii of what thing they must give account . Iii . to who this account must be make . IV. when it must be make . first , the person that must give account , our saviour Christ express show we to be man . m•t . 13.36 . but i say unto you ( say our saviour ) that of every idle word that man shall speak , they shall give account at the day of judgement . and S. Paul say , we must all appear before the tribunal seat of Christ , 1 Cor. 5.30 . that every man may receive accord to his work . for as Lingua est mentis interpres , the tongue be the interpreter of the mind , so say our saviour , by thy word ( if thou do well ) thou shall be justify , Mat 12.37 . and by thy word ( if thou do evil ) thou shall be condemn . thus we may see Christ true and just judgement , how that first by the evidence of their deed they shall be accuse : second , by the witness of their word convict : and three , by the testimony of their own conscience condemn , thus all man , none except , of all age , and of all nation , king and prince , rich and poor , yea all that have be from the begin of the world , and shall be to the end of the same , shall appear before the tribunal seat of Christ , and give a account every one for himself . two of what thing they must give account . Mat 12.29 2 Cor. 5.10 Eccl. 12.4 . Wisd. 1.9 . Mat 12.37 Rom. 4.12 . 1 Pet. 4.5 . they shall give account not only of every idle word , work , and deed , but also of every idle thought : yea , every man , and every member of man shall answer to his default . the king must give a account for himself , and how he have govern his kingdom . the minister that have take upon they curam animarum , the charge of soul , must give a account how they have feed their flock , and how they have live in their call : the magistrate for his justice : the master for his servant : the parent for the child , and every artificer for his trade , every man in his own proper person , must render a account of his talon , be it ten , four , or one that he have receive . and so straight shall this account be , that every member of every body shall answer to his default : the eye shall give a account what they have see : the ear what they have hear : the tongue what it have speak : the hand what they have feel : the foot where they have be : and the heart of man what it have think . to conclude , both heart and body , with all the part and power thereof shall be indict and arraign before the tribunal seat of Christ to render a strait account , tam de dictis quam de factis n'est , both of their word and their deed . Iii . to who this account must be give . the judge to who we must give our account be Christ . the lord command ( say David ) to judge the world . Psal. 56 13 John 9.22 . S. John say , •he father , judge no man , but have commit all judgement to the son . Gen. •8 25 and Abraham give God the stile of the judge of the world . there it be evident that our saviour Jesus Christ be judge of the world . he be verus Iudex and Iustus , he be a true and upright judge ? yea , Iames 4.12 he be a judge that be able to destroy both body and soul in hell fire for ever . Iud. 14.15 . but let we note , he come ▪ not alone , but bring ten thousand of his saint to execute judgement upon all ; yea , he say to his disciple , master 25.34 Wisd. 6. act 3 19 you which have follow i shall sit upon twelve throne , judge the twelve tribe of Israel . unto the godly he shall say , come you bless etc they shall both with body and soul receive a glorious kingdom and a beautiful crown from the lord hand : for with his right hand shall he cover they , rev. 21.6 . rev. 7 . 1• . Easie 15.4 . and with his arm he shall protect they , because it shall be a day of refresh , when all tear shall be wipe away from their eye , and all sorrow shall cease , and they shall live and reign with the lamb for ever . to the wicked ( Christ say ) go you curse . but first let we note , m•t . 15.4 Mat 7.17 psalm . 9.8 that he do not judge they after the manner of worldly judge , who knowledge may be deceive with false and sophistical plea ; who justice may be corrupt with fear , hatred , love and reward ; who anger may be pacify with pitiful word . oh fearefall and terrible will the sight of this judge be to the wicked : for his power shall be invincible , his anger implacable , his knowledge infallible and his justice inflexible . first , tyranny and cruelty shall not countervail his power : second , benignity of pitiful word shall not appease his anger : third , liberality , nor reward shall not bow his justice : four , subtlety of advocate shall not blind his knowledge . whereupon say saint Augustine Judex ille nec gratia praevenitur , nec misericordia flectitur , nec pecunia corrumpitur , nec penitentia mitigatur . neither be this judge prevent with favour , nor bow with mercy , nor bribe with money , neither mitigate by repentance : therefore when Christ say to the wicked , go you curse , he do not only curse they , but he bid they go , Mat 25 41. he send they to Gehenna , that dreadful dungeon of utter darkness , that burn lake and most fearful Tophet , yea , to that hollow cave and chaos of all confusion , to that bottomless pit of eternal perdition : who burn furnace be make by God justice , his power uphold it , and it be blow with the bellows of his wrath ever prepare for the devil and his angel . and as Christ say , go you curse , his curse be paenarum inflictio , infliction of punishment , which be twofold , paena damni and paena sensus , the punishment of loss , and the punishment of sense . the loss of God , of his glorious and most bless presence , his mercy , his favour , his bounty , his beauty , his grace , and all his eternal goodness , the loss of heaven , the habitation of God , and of all his holy and bless angel and saint , the house and heaven of happiness , with all the unspeakable joy , pleasure glory , riches , and treasure thereof for evermore . the punishment of sense which be hell and all the intolerable , everlasting , and woeful torment thereof . as the blessing of God comprehend all blessing , so this curse , anathema and Maranatha , include all cross , Mat 25.41 . go you curse , etc they shall be curse of Christ himself , curse shall they be of the angel , who curse be conscientiae cruciamen vexation of conscience . curse shall they be of the devil , who curse be paenarum executio , the execution of their punishment , accord to that of the poet : Minos examen , Radamanthus that cruciaman ; Tertius , beufrater terti aiura tenet . one devil rip up the examination , another devil torment , the three add one torment upon another , for their desire be revenge , their devotion curse , and their blessing blasphemy . they be in a sea of misery , and in a ocean of calamity ; fire flame about they ; the worm of conscience gnaw within they ; rage , madness , and ireful indignation among they , ugly visage of black and fearful devil affright they ; sulphur and hot burn coal under they : the revenge hand of God over they , and pour forth the vial of his wrath full of variety of plague without cease upon they .

they have penury , for gluttony : extreme thirst , for excessive drink : burn , for lecherous lust : want for ill get wealth : outrageous madness , for blaspheme : for oppression , utter desolation ; and a fearful agony for bloody cruelty . and S. Augustine speak of dive , say , augustines say of Dives , Rom. 7. desideravit guttam , qui non dedit micam , he will not give a erum , he shall not have a drop : a just recompense : as he deny the least comfort to Lazarus live , so Lazarus shall not bring he the least comfort dead . and Seneca say , nemini bone , sibi possimus , as he be good to none , so ( let it be his plague ) he be worst to himself ; thus the pain for sin answer the pleasure of sin . rev. 1.5 . Psal. 1.7 their torment shall be both comfortless and endless . they shall be die always , yet never dead . they shall seek death , but never find it . they shall be burn always , yet never burn to death . their meat shall be gripe hunger , Mat 13.42 Mat 22. 13 Psal. 11 , 7. and famine intolerable . their drink shall be lake of fire and brimstone . their music shall be howl and roar of foul and ugly devil , with savage fury accompany , and with barbarous torment cruel handle . thus heaven they shall have lose , which can be purchase . hell they have receive , rev. 18 , 2 and the place must needs be endure . and look how many sin they have set on the score , so many kind of punishment be provide for they in hell . o how many cause of weep and doleful cry shall those miserable wretch then have ? they shall weep because they can appeal from God dreadful judgement . they shall weep , because their pleasure have bring they to all these sorrow . they shall weep , because they shall see their misery be past all remedy , and their repentance too late . to conclude this point , they will then curse the parent which beget they , and the womb which bear they , the day wherein they be bear , and the air that give they breath : and will cry , woe , wo , that ever we be bear , oh where be that dive that will not believe this before he feel the fire of hell . IV. the time when we must give this account . we must give a account at the day of judgement , when Christ come to judge the world . but of that day and hour know no man , Mar. 13.3 . no not the angel which be in heaven ; neither the son accord to his humane nature ; but the father . but accord to his divine nature , he know as well as the father . therefore say Christ . it be not for you to know the time and season ; which the father have put in his own power . act 1.17 . but as Paul say to the Corinthians , see we be they upon who the end of the world be come , we ought to learn of our saviour , to watch , Mat 24.42 for we know not at what time the lord will come . and our saviour see the iniquity of the world grow so fast to maturity , say ; except those day shall be shorten , there shall not flesh be save . therefore say saint John , repent , for the kingdom of heaven be at hand . Mat 14.24 and S. Iames cry out , behold the judge stand before the door , if they look for it to be so nigh in those day , now sure it can be far off . Augustine in his book upon genesis , against the manichee say , that the world shall last six age . and ( accord to his prophecy ) we live in the last age . Eliah also prophesy that the world shall last six thousand year , two thousand before the law , two thousand under the law . and two thousand from Christ his birth until his come to judgement . if his prophecy be true , the world can last four hundred year . to conclude , let we be ready always howsoever , although this day be like yesterday , and too morrow like to day , yet let we not defer our repentance .
